entries.context: Common mild insult used when someone makes a dumb mistake. Heard frequently in Parisian traffic.
Very strong insult hurled in road rage or confrontations. Equivalent to a severe English swear.
entries.suggested_response: Do not engage. Walk away. This person is highly agitated.

entries.context: Used when criticizing someone's poor judgment. Commonly heard in arguments.
entries.suggested_response: Stay calm. A simple 'Je suis désolé' (I'm sorry) often diffuses the situation.

entries.context: An insult targeting someone's intelligence. Used in frustration or mockery.
entries.suggested_response: Ignore and move on. Responding invites further confrontation.

entries.context: Extreme profanity used to dismiss someone aggressively. Signals high anger.
entries.suggested_response: Leave the area immediately if safe. This person is extremely agitated.

entries.context: Mocking someone's ability or competence. Common in sports, driving disputes.
entries.suggested_response: Ignore or give a polite smile.

entries.context: Used when someone acts thoughtlessly or rudely. More forceful than 'nul'.
entries.suggested_response: Calmly say 'Je ne comprends pas' (I don't understand) and walk away.
